_note: This file drives the ENTIRE pricing page (website/src/pages/pricing.astro). Every visible string lives here — nothing is hardcoded in the component. Edit this file to change the hero, prices, plans, chips, CTAs, the comparison tables, or the FAQ. Structure: `hero` = the eyebrow/headline/description at the top of the page; `hostingToggle` = the two-option Cloud/Self-hosted switch; `plansByMode` = the plan cards shown for each toggle option (cloud = 4 cards, selfHosted = 2 cards); `comparisonByMode` = the comparison table shown for each toggle option, rendered server-side for BOTH modes and swapped by the same toggle (cloud = 4 plan columns, selfHosted = 2 plan columns); `faqs` = the FAQ accordion; `links` = a lookup that turns any occurrence of a `text` token in a feature, comparison label, comparison cell, or FAQ answer into a link to `href` (this is how the visible strings stay the source of truth while still linking — no link text is hardcoded in the components). In `comparisonByMode[mode].groups[].rows[].cells`, `true` renders a check, `false` renders a dash, and a string renders as-is. Plan card `chip` is a small pill that straddles the card's top border (null = no chip); `cta.style` is `primary` (yellow) or `outline`. Card chrome is uniform across every plan. Adding or removing a feature keeps the CTA divider aligned across a row automatically (the grid uses subgrid row tracks, so no per-card heights to tune).

Build and use agents through chat

faqs.question: What counts as an agent run?
faqs.answer: An agent run is any tracked execution that creates a trace. Sending a message to an agent, creating an annotation, or starting an agent from a schedule or event each creates one agent run and one trace. Each new message, annotation, or trigger is a separate run.

faqs.question: Is an agent run the same as a trace?
faqs.answer: An agent run is the customer-facing usage unit. A trace is the technical record Agenta creates for that run so you can inspect its model calls, tool calls, timing, outputs, and errors. Every agent run creates one trace.

faqs.question: Are users, projects, agents, and workflows really unlimited?
faqs.answer: Yes, on every paid Cloud plan and both self-hosted plans. Invite as many team members as you need and create as many projects, agents, workflows, schedules, and triggers as you want. The free Cloud Hobby plan includes two team members. You pay based on agent runs, not seats, projects, agents, steps, or workflows.

faqs.question: Are AI model costs included?
faqs.answer: No. Connect your own model-provider credentials and pay the provider directly.

faqs.question: Which model providers can I use?
faqs.answer: Connect OpenAI, Anthropic, Gemini, Mistral, Groq, MiniMax, Together AI, OpenRouter, Azure OpenAI, AWS Bedrock, or Google Vertex AI. You can also connect an OpenAI-compatible endpoint.

faqs.question: What happens when I reach my usage limit?
faqs.answer: Hobby stops running agents after 5,000 agent runs in a monthly billing period. Upgrade to Pro to continue. Pro and Business include 10,000 agent runs per month. Additional runs are billed automatically at $5 per 10,000, and your agents continue running without interruption.

faqs.question: Do unused agent runs roll over?
faqs.answer: No. Included agent runs reset at the start of each monthly billing period.

faqs.question: Where can I monitor usage?
faqs.answer: Open Settings, then Billing to see your current agent-run usage and plan allowance.

faqs.question: How can I estimate how many agent runs I need?
faqs.answer: Count each message sent to an agent, each annotation, and each invocation started by a schedule or event. For example, one daily scheduled agent uses about 30 or 31 runs per month, one agent triggered every hour uses about 720 to 744 runs per month, and a chat with 20 user messages uses 20 runs.

faqs.question: What does trace data retention cover?
faqs.answer: Trace data retention controls how long Agenta Cloud keeps technical execution records, including model calls, tool calls, timing, outputs, errors, evaluations, and annotations. Hobby retains trace data for one week, Pro for one month, and Business for three months. Enterprise retention is configurable. Trace data retention does not describe the retention of chats, project files, or persistent agent context. With self-hosted Agenta, you manage trace storage and retention in your own infrastructure.

faqs.question: What is the difference between Agenta Cloud and self-hosted Agenta?
faqs.answer: Agenta Cloud is operated by us. We run the application, storage, agent runner, upgrades, and backups. With self-hosted Agenta, you deploy and operate those components in your own infrastructure, connect your own model and integration accounts, and control where your data is stored.

faqs.question: What is included in the free open-source edition?
faqs.answer: The open-source edition includes the core agent workspace: chat, projects, agent configuration, instructions, skills, tools, integrations, MCP servers, files, schedules, event triggers, tracing, evaluations, version history, role-based access control, and SSO. You can create unlimited users, projects, agents, and workflows on your own infrastructure.

faqs.question: Why pay for Enterprise if Agenta is open source?
faqs.answer: Enterprise adds audit logs, custom domains, deployment and upgrade support, priority support, a private Slack Connect channel, dedicated support, and a custom service-level agreement. The open-source edition remains free for teams that can operate it themselves and do not need those commercial features or services.

faqs.question: Can I switch plans or cancel at any time?
faqs.answer: Yes. Upgrade, downgrade, or cancel from Settings, then Billing.

faqs.question: Do you offer discounts for startups, students, or researchers?
faqs.answer: Yes. Early-stage startups, students, and researchers can email team@agenta.ai to ask about discounted or free access. Include what you are building and how you plan to use Agenta.

faqs.question: Does Agenta offer a free trial?
faqs.answer: Agenta does not use a time-limited free trial. Start with the free Hobby plan on Agenta Cloud or deploy the free open-source edition on your own infrastructure.
